preview.summary: Senegal close out Group I against a stubborn Iraq side at BMO Field, with positioning behind France potentially at stake. The reigning African champions bring a deep, talented squad and attacking pace, while Iraq lean on defensive intensity, physicality and set-piece threat. A patient Senegal will look to break down a disciplined, hard-working opponent.
preview.homeForm: Ranked around 14th by FIFA, fielding a star-studded squad under Pape Thiaw.
preview.awayForm: Ranked roughly 57th and the group's lowest-ranked team, back at the World Cup after a 40-year wait.
preview.homeKey: Sadio Mané, the captain and all-time top scorer in his last World Cup
preview.awayKey: Aymen Hussein, the talisman striker and qualifying matchwinner
storylines: Mané's farewell World Cup as Senegal's leader
Iraq's first World Cup appearance since 1986
Senegal's golden generation chasing a deep run
preview.h2h: The sides have met rarely, with no significant competitive history between them.
